$comment: MCP tool contract for `verify`. inputSchema/outputSchema are what the server advertises in its tool catalog. outputSchema is the AGGREGATED tool result; the per-pass model schema is the same shape minus `confidence`/`passes` (see data-model.md §3-4). The sanitized (Anthropic-grammar) form is derived from this by the schema sanitizer at startup; the constraints below that the grammar cannot enforce (minimum/maximum on confidence, minLength on claim) are enforced by the local validator.
name: verify
description: Independently verify a claim. Runs multiple parallel verification passes that see only the claim and optional context - never the requester's stance or conversation. Returns a structured verdict: supported or refuted, specific concrete findings (every refutation names the exact error), and a confidence score derived from cross-pass agreement. Use when an assertion matters and being confidently wrong is costly.

claim.description: The claim to verify, stated neutrally.

context.description: Optional supporting context the verifier may consult. This is the only extra information a verification pass receives.

verdict.description: Majority verdict across passes; ties resolve to refuted.

findings.description: Deduplicated specific findings from the majority-side passes. Non-empty whenever verdict is refuted.

confidence.description: Agreement ratio (majority count / passes). Computed by the server, never model self-report.

passes.description: Number of verification passes that completed.
